content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, read the changed config/policy/diagnostics paths and related tests end-to-end, ran `cargo test`, and spot-checked the CLI in a fresh temp workspace. The default implementer/reviewer resolution itself looks sound, but the backend diagnostics still misreport final-review reviewers after the new `final_review.backends` default.

## Amendments

1. Fix the final-review diagnostics to report the new reviewer defaults from `final_review.backends`. The compiled default panel now comes from `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:266-270`, but `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:785-824` still renders the `final_reviewer` row by calling `resolve_role_target(FinalReviewer, 1)`, and `src/contexts/agent_execution/policy.rs:60-76` resolves that role through the planner-family fallback instead of the explicit final-review panel. In a clean workspace this produces a contradictory result: `backend show-effective --json` reports only `final_reviewer = claude/claude-opus-4-6`, while `backend probe --role final_review_panel --flow standard --json` correctly resolves the panel as `codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh` plus `claude/claude-opus-4-6`. Update `show-effective` (and singular `final_reviewer` probes if they remain supported via `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs:870-884`) to surface the actual `final_review.backends` members, or stop exposing a generic `final_reviewer` role that no longer matches runtime behavior.
   Rationale: This is a user-visible correctness gap in the diagnostics surface after the default change: operators checking effective config are shown the wrong final-review reviewer set, which makes the new cross-model default look absent or misconfigured.
